                          Search Results

                            Characters | Expedition 33 Wiki


                            Characters in Clair Obscur Expedition 33 are playable characters that possess unique traits, abilities, and backstories that distinguish them from one another. Players can choose which Characters to bring on their expedition across the different areas of The Continent, with players strategically giving commands to the Characters during turn-based combat. Each Character comes with their own set of customizable and upgradeable equipment, allowing players to tailor gear for optimal party synergy and composition. There are 6 Party Members in Clair Obscur Expedition 33.

                            An active party can consist of up to 3 characters. If all party members in the active party have fallen in battle, players will have the option to send out the reserved members to continue the battle. Reserved party members cannot revive previously fallen party members, and if the reserved party members also fall in battle, the game is over, and you'll reload the game at the last save. In combat, Active party members gain more XP after winning a combat encounter, but reserved party members will also acquire some XP. The max character level for the characters is Level 99.

                            NOTE: Character information for Maelle and Verso contains HEAVY SPOILERS. It is recommended to read them thoroughly only after Act II Finale. 

                             

                            All Clair Obscur Expedition 33 Characters Guide

                            Clair Obscur Expedition 33 Characters Quick Navigate

                            Click on the character's name to find an overview of the character.

                            Gustave ♦ Lune ♦ Maelle ♦ Sciel ♦ Verso ♦ Monoco

                             

                            Expedition 33 Behind the Scenes Cast Interview

                             

                            Clair Obscur Expedition 33 Beginner Guide

                             

                             

                            How to Unlock New Skills and Get Skill Points?

                            Gain XP by defeating Enemies and Bosses to level up your characters. When a character has reached a new level, your character will be granted 1 Skill Point and 3 Attribute Points. Proceed to look for an Expedition Flag, and choose the option "Learn Skill" to use your skill point on a skill you desire to unlock.

                            Each skill will vary in cost as seen on their skill tree. You can also respec all spent Skill Points by using a Recoat. As you progress through the story of the game, you will learn Gradient Skills, unique skills that are unlocked for each character by increasing their Relationship Levels at Camp. The first Gradient Skill is unlocked when the combat mechanic becomes available in Act II, a character unlocks their second gradient skill at Relationship Level 4, while the third gradient skill is unlocked upon reaching max Relationship Level 7.

                            Gradient Skills require Gradient Charges, which can be charged by attacking, using skills, and equipping Pictos or Luminas that can provide passive effects to charge up the Gradient Bar. Using a Gradient Skill does not cost a turn and only consumes gradient charges.

                             

                            How to Upgrade Weapons in Expedition 33

                            Upgrading weapons in Clair Obscur Expedition 33 features a unique mechanic. There are two ways that a weapon can be upgraded to a higher level.

                            1. Some specific enemy or boss encounters will reward the player with weapon upgrades to specific weapons, provided that you have already acquired the weapon. These specific enemy encounters can be within Locations or on The Continent. These upgrades are only rewarded once or after winning the initial battle encounter.
                            2. The traditional way of upgrading weapons is by interacting with The Curator at Camp. As you progress through the game, you can use any Expedition Flag to upgrade weapons.

                            Upgrading Weapons and Resources requires Tints (or Materials), these can be acquired in various ways, such as loot, occasional random drops from defeating enemies, unique (one-time) drops from defeating Bosses or special enemies that do not respawn, purchased from Gestral Merchants, or acquired as reward for completing side quests.

                            For Upgrading Weapons, the following Tints are required:

                            • Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 3.
                            • Polished Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 9.
                            • Resplendent Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 19.
                            • Grandiose Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 32.
                            • Perfect Chroma Catalyst: Can be used at Camp to upgrade weapons up to level 33.

                            How to Upgrade Pictos in Expedition 33?

                            Upgrading Pictos in Clair Obscur Expedition 33 features a unique mechanic. Some specific enemy or boss encounters will reward the player with pictos upgrades to a specific Pictos, provided that you have already acquired the Pictos.

                            These specific enemy encounters can be within Locations or on The Continent. These upgrades are only rewarded once or after winning the initial battle encounter. When a Pictos level is upgraded, the stat values for the combat stats of that Pictos will improve. Please see our Interactive Map to find all enemy encounters that can drop or provide free upgrades for the Pictos.

                             

                            How to Get More Lumina Points in Expedition 33?

                            The traditional method for increasing the Lumina Points for each character is by leveling up. However, you can use a Tint item, the Colour of Lumina, to increase the number of Lumina Points. Adding Lumina Points only costs 1 Colour of Lumina per Lumina Point added to the character. These cannot be respec and are permanent. So use them wisely.

                            To upgrade Lumina Points, you can interact with The Curator at Camp and choose the option to upgrade the Expeditioner's Resources. As you progress through the game, you can use any Expedition Flag to upgrade the expedition's resources.

                            How and Where do I get Colour of Lumina in Clair Obscur Expedition 33?

                            Colour of Lumina can be acquired in various ways, such as loot, occasional random drops from defeating enemies, unique (one-time) drops from defeating Bosses or special enemies that do not respawn, purchased from Gestral Merchants, or acquired as reward for completing side quests.

                             

                            All Characters in Clair Obscur Expedition 33

                            Gustave

                            "For those who come after."

                            • Weapon Type: Sword & Pistol
                            • Starting Weapon: Noahram
                            • Starting Skills: Lumiere Assault, Overcharge

                            Gustave is a resourceful and dedicated Engineer who volunteers to join Expedition 33. He devotes his final year to defeating the Paintress and to reclaim the future for the people of Lumiere. His worldview has been shaped by having lived his entire life with the threat of the Paintress' presence over Lumière. He is both the foster father and brother of Maelle.

                            You begin the game playing as Gustave, making your way out of the forest with the overwhelming feeling of hopelessness. Until you find Lune, she encourages you and joins you on your journey.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Gustave

                            Gustave Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 150
                            • Attack Power: 49
                            • Speed: 212
                            • Defense: 0
                            • Critical Rate: 8%

                            Gustave wields a gun and a sword and is the first playable character in this game. The pistol is a good weapon to aim and shoot at an enemy's weak points when in combat. Take note that shooting costs 1 AP. That being said, his attacks can be basic but powerful with the right upgrades. He starts with 2 skills, Lumiere Assault and Overcharge. Lumiere deals low physical damage with 5 hits, but the critical hit will generate an additional charge for his Overcharge.

                            Overcharge deals high lightning damage based on the number of his charges per hit. To charge up the skill or his bionic arm, you must attack an enemy or use Skills that can generate charges. Normal attacks yield 1 Charge, while other skills can vary, such as providing Gustave with more than 1 Charge. Players can also notice that Gustave's bionic arm will change color as it gains Charges.

                            At max Overcharge (10 Charges), Lumiere's arm will glow red, which is the recommended time to use Overcharge to deliver a devastating Lightning damage that can also Break the target. By using Overcharge, no matter the number of charges you've gathered, using the skill will reset all Charges back to zero.

                             

                            Lune

                            "When one falls, we continue."

                            • Weapon Type: Elemental Magic
                            • Starting Weapon: Lunerim
                            • Starting Skills: Immolation, Ice Lance

                            Lune is a passionate Scholar and Mage who is in charge of charting a path for the 33rd Expedition. This gives her the weight of responsibility to make sure that the Expedition does not fail. She is the daughter of two prominent researchers, and she inherits her deep thirst for knowledge and discoveries. That being said, she will make sure and will sacrifice everything in order to complete her parents' work.

                            Lune is the first character you find while exploring as Gustave at the beginning of the game. She accompanies Gustave to satisfy her curiosity about the Paintress. A unique detail about Lune is that when sprinting, instead of running on the ground, she will levitate and glide towards the direction you are controlling her in.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Lune

                            Lune Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 150
                            • Attack Power: 51
                            • Speed: 200
                            • Defense: 0
                            • Critical Rate: 5%

                            Lune is an Elemental Mage, you will need to use her skills to generate Stains. Lune's Skills can help generate Elemental Stains, it can also consume her Stains to enhance the effectiveness of her Skills. She has 5 types of Stains: Ice Stains, Fire Stains, Lightning Stains, Earth Stains, and Light Stains. Light Stains are special as they can be consumed regardless of the element that is needed. It is best to cycle between the Elemental Stains to get the most out of her skills.

                            Lune's starting skills are Immolation and Ice Lance. Using Ice Lance will generate 1 Ice Stain and one Light Stain, while using a Fire Skill like Immolation will consume Ice Stains. Information like this will be listed in the description of the skill to avoid confusion.

                            Lune can also inflict Elemental Affinities onto enemies, most enemies have Elemental Affinities that you will have to find and exploit to optimize the damage that you deal to them. The 4 Elemental Affinities are as follows:

                            • Weakness: Takes 50% more damage.
                            • Resistance: Takes 50% less damage.
                            • Nullify: Takes no damage.
                            • Absorb: Heals instead of taking damage.

                             

                            Maelle

                            "I've had enough condolences."

                            • Weapon Type: Rapier
                            • Starting Weapon: Maellum
                            • Starting Skills: Offensive Switch, Percee, Spark

                            Maelle is the overarching protagonist of Expedition 33 and the foster sister/daughter of Gustave, though she is more of a shy loner, but is eager to fly; her reasoning to join the 33rd Expedition at the age of 16 is to explore the world outside of Lumiere. She was orphaned at the young age of 3 and has never felt like she fit in anywhere in Lumiere, but has a deep appreciation and trust towards Gustave. Maelle is in reality the canvas reincarnation of Alicia Dessendre after she enters with the goal of stopping Aline and Renoir from fighting over the fate of the canvas. Maelle herself wishes the Canvas world to remain intact and after becoming a paintress, she gains the goal of resurrecting everyone and stopping her father and her painted brother from destroying it.

                            Maelle is the third character you find. Gustave is eager to find Maelle when he hears that she is alive, but Lune believes it might be a trap.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Maelle

                            Maelle Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 195
                            • Attack Power: 105
                            • Speed: 212
                            • Defense: 0
                            • Critical Rate: 5%

                            Maelle wields a Rapier, and similarly to Lune, Maelle's Skills change her stances, which are the following:

                            • Stanceless: No special effects. Usually starts in this stance.
                            • Defensive Stance: Takes less damage and gains 1 AP per Parry or Dodge.
                            • Offensive Stance: Deals 50% more damage but takes 50% increased damage.
                            • Virtuose Stance: Deals 200% damage.

                            Activating and entering into a different Stance grants Maelle 1 AP, so switching between her stances without getting interrupted maximizes the effects of her skills. Otherwise, she will switch back to Stanceless after the skill. Maelle can also switch between Defensive and Offensive Stances by using a basic attack.

                            Maelle's starting Skills are Offensive Switch, Percee, and Spark. Performing Offensive Switch puts Maelle in the Offensive Stance and applies Defenceless, which makes the target take 25% more damage. Performing Spark or Percee will put Maelle in the Defensive Stance. However, while in Offensive Stance or Virtuose Stance grants additional effects when performing Spark.

                            Several of Maelle's skills apply or are boosted by Burn, so it is highly effective to have her equip weapons or pictos that can improve or apply other effects/bonuses whenever she lands attacks or skills that burn enemies. Maelle is also a character that is effective against enemies that have shields, as one of her skills can instantly break all the shields, gain AP depending on the number of shields broken on the enemy, and can also immediately play a free turn if she uses the skill against an enemy that is inflicted with Defenceless.

                             

                            Sciel

                            "Death is a friend who will welcome me home."

                            • Weapon Type: Scythe
                            • Starting Weapon: Scieleson
                            • Starting Skills: Focused Foretell, Twilight Slash

                            Sciel is a cheerful yet serene Warrior who wields a Scythe and has put all her commitment into the Expedition to make sure they do not fail. Growing up, Sciel was a farmer until she became a teacher. Her carefree personality allows her to enjoy life and accept the fate she will have at the hands of the Paintress.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Sciel

                            Sciel Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 318
                            • Attack Power: 273
                            • Speed: 251
                            • Defense: 0
                            • Critical Rate: 5%

                            Sciel's playstyle is similar to a push-and-pull concept where players must strategically use Skills to apply enemies with Foretell  and consume the Foretell  to increase the effectiveness of Sciel's skills. Sciel can apply and consume Foretell  on enemies (Max of 10 per enemy). Some skills state that using it will apply Foretell  while others will consume Foretell . Skills that apply Foretell  generate 1 Sun Charge, while Skills that consume Foretell  give Sciel 1 Moon Charge. Sun and Moon Charges will stack depending on which Skill you use. It is important to read the effects of her skills since each can trigger extra effects if conditions are met. For example, if Focused Foretell is used against an enemy that doesn't have Foretell ,  the enemy acquires 5 Foretell  instead of 2.

                            Now, when combining a Sun Charge and a Moon Charge, these combine, and Sciel's Foretell changes to  Twilight. At the start of Sciel's turn, she will enter Twilight for a duration of 2 Turns. While in  Twilight:

                            • Sciel applies double Foretell 
                            • The maximum amount of Foretell per enemy increases from 10 to 20.
                            • Damage is significantly increased depending on the number of Sun and Moon charges consumed.

                            So, while Sciel is in Twilight, it is highly recommended to maximize this effect before it ends. Once Twilight ends, all Sun and Moon charges will go back to zero, and you'll need to repeat the process of using skills to generate charges until you see fit that it's time to switch into Twilight. When upgrading her attributes, increasing her Luck and Agility is highly recommended. Sciel's skills center around dealing multiple hits, and you can further increase its damage by applying and consuming Foretell  and maximizing the effects of  Twilight, increasing her luck and agility will allow you to have a high chance of landing critical hits and getting to play as much as often.

                             

                            Verso

                            "Our Whole World Carries The Burden Of Your Family's Grief."

                            • Weapon Type: Sword & Dagger
                            • Starting Weapon: Verleso
                            • Starting Skills: Assault Zero, From Fire

                            Verso is a member of Expedition Zero. He wanders The Continent and keeps a close eye on the surviving members of Expedition 33. Verso is an experienced fighter and can fight against any threat that comes his way. Verso joins Expedition 33 to achieve the goal of stopping the Paintress from ever painting again. He is a member of the Painted Dessendre family, created by Aline, The Paintress, as a coping mechanism for her grief. Real Verso created the Canvas, the world where Expedition 33 takes place. He wishes to destroy the Canvas and everyone in it in an attempt to give his family closure and put his soul to rest.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Verso

                            Verso Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 763
                            • Attack Power: 666
                            • Speed: 374
                            • Defense: 71
                            • Critical Rate: 14%

                            Verso's Perfection: Verso gains Perfection every time he deals damage (Damaging Skills, Basic Attack, Free Aim Shot) or manages to Parry or Dodge. A higher Perfection Rank increases the damage or unlocks additional effects of a skill. Some Skills have additional effects at specific Perfection Ranks. Taking any damage removes 1 Perfection Rank (once per enemy turn), so it is vital to Dodge or Parry as much as you can to keep increasing the Rank or maintain the current rank Verso has acquired.

                            At the start of a battle, Verso starts at, and attacking and successful dodges or parrying will add Perfection Points to increase his Perfection Rank to >  >  > and . When playing Verso, it is best to have him equip Weapons or Pictos that can grant him extra turns or extra attacks. This way, you can maximize each of his turns to rank up faster. The higher the rank, the more effective and strong Verso's skills become.

                            One weapon in particular that is highly recommended for him to use is the Dualiso weapon. At Weapon Lvl. 4, the weapon allows Verso to immediately play an extra turn after using a basic attack. A good combo to use when equipping this weapon is to use a Free Aim Shot, a Basic Attack, another Free Aim Shot, and finish your turn with another Basic Attack or a Skill to increase Verso's Perfection Rank by the end of his turn.

                            When upgrading his attributes, increasing his Luck, Agility, and Might are what's effective for Verso. Increasing the chances of landing critical hits, the damage output of his attacks, and getting to have more turns in battle is vital as you want Verso to attack as much as possible to get to max perfection rank.

                             

                            Monoco

                            "My, what lovely feet."

                            • Weapon Type: Staff
                            • Starting Weapon: Monocaro
                            • Starting Skills: Chalier Combo, Stalact Punches

                            Monoco is a Gestral, a group of friendly beings who enjoy the thrill of battle and view competition as a form of meditation. As one of the few Gestrals who speak the human language, Monoco has adopted a scholarly demeanour which belies a bloodthirsty spirit. Though Gestrals are untouched by the Paintress, the prospect of combat entices Monoco to join the Expedition.

                             

                            Combat Overview for Monoco

                            Monoco Starting Attributes

                            • Health: 509
                            • Attack Power: 491
                            • Speed: 304
                            • Defense: 0
                            • Critical Rate: 5%

                            Monoco is a unique Gestral that has the ability to shapeshift into a Nevron and use its skills. For Monoco to learn his Skills, he must be in an active party and defeat enemies that have this unique icon  next to the enemy's health bar. Upon defeating the enemy, Monoco will acquire the foot of said enemy and unlock a new skill for his arsenal.

                            Moving to the unique combat mechanic, Monoco features a Bestial Wheel, Monoco's skills will have designated "turns" that will move the Bestial Wheel pointer forward upon using the skill. The Bestial Wheel features various masks, such as  Agile Mask,  Caster Mask,  Balanced Mask,  Heavy Mask, and the  Almighty Mask.

                            Each Skill of Monoco is tied to specific Masks, which means that before using a skill, players should look at where the Bestial Wheel Pointer is currently on to maximize the effects of the skill. For example one of Monoco's staring skills is Stalact Punches, which is tied to a  Heavy Mask, players can use this at any time as long as he has enough AP, but it's more effective to use the skill when the Bestial Wheel Pointer is on a  Heavy Mask to further increase its damage. On the other hand, the  Almighty Mask is an "all-round mask" which also provides the same effect by buffing all of Monoco's skills.
